prompt:
Implement a Regulatory Operations & Analytics Portal for an on-premise professional services organization that must manage credentialed practitioners, appointments, controlled content, and auditable analytics without any internet dependency. The React web interface presents role-based navigation for Standard Users, Content Admins, Credential Reviewers, Analysts, and System Admins, with immediate inline validation and clear status feedback for every action. Users register and sign in with a username and password, then maintain practitioner profiles centered on lawyer identity, firm affiliation, and license details, attaching credential files (PDF/JPG/PNG) and submitting them into a review queue where reviewers can approve, reject with a required comment, or request resubmission while preserving the full version history. Scheduling is handled through a calendar-style workbench where administrators configure weekly availability and slot duration (default 30 minutes), and users book time slots with real-time conflict detection and “held” states; bookings cannot be made more than 90 days ahead, rescheduling is allowed up to 2 times per appointment, and cancellations inside 24 hours are blocked unless performed by a System Admin. Content Admins manage a question bank for standardized intake and internal assessment, supporting rich text with formulas and embedded images, bulk Excel/CSV import/export, tagging and difficulty levels, draft/published/offline status, and duplicate detection that flags questions with high textual similarity for review before publishing. Analysts use an analytics visualization workbench to run unified queries, generate reusable tags/features, sample datasets, and export results to CSV, alongside KPI dashboards with trend, distribution, and correlation views and filters for date range and organizational unit; compliance dashboards include operational KPIs such as rescue volume, recovery rate, adoption conversion, average shelter stay, donation mix, and supply turnover, with one-click report export for audits.

The backend uses Symfony to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the React client, enforcing consistent authorization at both menu and API levels and persisting all business data in MySQL with strict transactional integrity. Appointment inventory deduction must be concurrency-safe using database transactions and row-level locking to prevent overbooking, and conflict rules must reject overlapping bookings for the same practitioner and location. Data governance requirements include immutable audit logs for logins, approvals, imports/exports, and record changes, with sensitive-field access logs and retention for 7 years; versioning and rollback are required for credential documents and question content, with rollbacks restricted to System Admins and always requiring step-up confirmation (re-enter password) plus a justification note. Data quality validation runs on save and on bulk import, enforcing required fields, ranges (e.g., difficulty 1–5), and cross-field consistency, and generating local anomaly alerts in an admin console when thresholds are exceeded (for example, more than 5 rejected credentials in 24 hours for the same firm). Security controls include server-side input validation, prepared statements to prevent injection, CSRF protection, upload limits of 10 MB per file with MIME/type allowlists, password hashing with bcrypt, field-level encryption for license numbers using AES-256, masking in the UI by default, and login failure lockout after 5 attempts for 15 minutes with a locally generated CAPTCHA; password recovery is handled via admin-initiated reset only. A reserved “human-verification” integration point may be configured for future on-prem deployments but must be disabled by default and must not require any external network connectivity.

stack: Frontend Stack: React

Backend Stack: Symfony (REST-style APIs)

Database Stack: MySQL
